scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test parsing multiple valid lines and returning a list of dictionaries.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function correctly parses multiple data lines, validates constraints, and returns a list.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The returned list must contain one dictionary per data line, each with correct parsed values and mu sum = 1, C and p* lengths equal.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed code returns a single dictionary or does not validate mu sum or length.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.6\t2:3\t0.1,0.2\n"
    "15.3\t0.5,0.5\t2:3\t0.3,0.2\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Modify the input file to contain multiple data lines, requiring the function to parse all lines and return a list of dictionaries.
Add numeric constraints that mu values must sum to 1 within tolerance, and that the number of copy number states equals the number of probabilities; raise ValueError if violated.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test that a line with mu sum not equal to 1 raises ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function raises ValueError when mu values do not sum to 1 within tolerance.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: For any line, mu_normal + sum(mu_tumors) must equal 1 within 1e-9 tolerance; otherwise ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed code accepts invalid mu sums silently.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.7\t2:3\t0.1,0.2\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Add numeric constraints that mu values must sum to 1 within tolerance, and that the number of copy number states equals the number of probabilities; raise ValueError if violated.

stdout_contains: ValueError: mu values do not sum to 1

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test that a line with mismatched lengths between C and p* raises ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function raises ValueError when flattened C length does not equal flattened p* length.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: After flattening, the number of copy number entries must equal the number of probability entries; otherwise ValueError.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed code accepts mismatched lengths silently.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = (
    "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n"
    "12.5\t0.4,0.6\t2:3\t0.1,0.2,0.3\n"
)
fname = "theta_test.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Add numeric constraints that mu values must sum to 1 within tolerance, and that the number of copy number states equals the number of probabilities; raise ValueError if violated.
